note: Single-pole eddy model (Ch. 6 tau_e): the conductive secondary field is QUADRATURE-dominated at low omega*tau (phase -> -90 deg; Q/I = 3.3 at omega*tau=0.3) and rotates to in-phase (anti, -180 deg) at high omega*tau. A FERROMAGNETIC distorter is reactive and in-phase (Q ~ 0). So at a typical EMT operating point (omega*tau<1) the QUADRATURE channel of the lock-in flags CONDUCTIVE distortion selectively while ferromagnetic distortion is invisible in Q (caught only by the in-phase residual). Phase-sensitive detect-and-flag, near-free from the dual-phase lock-in (Ch. 20 §20.10, Ch. 27).
